sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Array Technologies, Inc. (ARRY) Expands Solar Tracker Business with Record Order Book
feed.summary: Array Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ:ARRY) recently announced strong financial results, with a record $2.2 billion order book and 35% annual volume growth, despite a decrease in gross margin. The company plans to introduce new products and strategic imperatives to innovate and elevate its international business. Following these results, Jefferies lowered its price target for ARRY, citing concerns that management's focus on backlog growth might impact margins.

feed.title: BCE Files 2025 Annual Report on Form 40-F with U.S. SEC
feed.summary: BCE Inc. has filed its 2025 annual report on Form 40-F with the U.S. SEC, including audited financial statements for the year ended December 31, 2025. This filing ensures transparency and compliance for U.S. investors, supporting BCE's presence in U.S. capital markets. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates BCE stock as Neutral due to a mixed financial profile despite attractive valuation and supportive technical momentum.

feed.title: SHAREHOLDER ACTION REMINDER: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Reminds Apollo Global Management (APO) Investors of Securities Class Action Deadline on May 1, 2026
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP has issued a reminder to investors of Apollo Global Management (APO) regarding a securities class action lawsuit. The lawsuit's deadline for lead plaintiff applications is May 1, 2026. This notice serves to inform affected shareholders about their rights and the process to potentially recover losses.

feed.title: Coterra Energy (NYSE:CTRA) Downgraded to "Hold" Rating by Siebert Williams Shank
feed.summary: Siebert Williams Shank has downgraded Coterra Energy (NYSE:CTRA) from a "buy" to a "hold" rating, though the stock still maintains a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $33.95. The energy company's stock opened at $31.54, near its 52-week high, with a market capitalization of $23.95 billion. Institutional investors and hedge funds hold a significant 87.92% of the company's stock.

feed.title: AvalonBay Communities (NYSE:AVB) Price Target Lowered to $202.00 at Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has lowered its price target for AvalonBay Communities (NYSE:AVB) from $217 to $202, while maintaining an "overweight" rating, suggesting a 12.8% upside. Other analysts like Wall Street Zen, Mizuho, RBC, and Piper Sandler have also trimmed their targets or ratings, leading to a MarketBeat consensus of "Hold" with an average price target of $199.66. Despite these adjustments, AvalonBay recently beat EPS estimates and institutional investors own a significant portion of the company's stock.

feed.title: Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) Given New $44.00 Price Target at Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has raised its price target for Fastenal (NASDAQ:FAST) to $44.00 from $43.00, maintaining an "equal weight" rating, despite suggesting a potential 6% downside from its previous close. The analyst consensus for Fastenal remains a "Hold" with an average price target of $48.83. The company recently reported Q4 earnings per share of $0.26, meeting expectations, and revenue of $2.03 billion, which was up 11.1% year-over-year but slightly below consensus estimates.

feed.title: The Cooper Companies (COO) Stock: Falls Despite Strong Q1 Revenue and 20% EPS Growth
feed.summary: The Cooper Companies (COO) reported strong fiscal first-quarter results with revenue up 6.2% to $1.024 billion and non-GAAP EPS increasing 20% to $1.10. Despite raising full-year guidance and experiencing growth in its CooperVision and CooperSurgical segments, the stock fell over 2% and continued to decline in pre-market trading. The downturn occurred even as premium lens sales drove CooperVision's growth and CooperSurgical showed improved demand stabilization and strong free cash flow generation.

feed.title: Ford’s new battery business seeks to spin profits out of costly EV investments
feed.summary: Ford is establishing a new battery business to leverage its investments in electric vehicle (EV) battery production. The company aims to generate profits from these ventures by producing battery cells for both residential energy storage solutions and EVs, starting with its Marshall, Michigan plant in 2026. This strategic move is intended to recoup the significant costs associated with EV development.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark names Francesco Tinto as chief information & GBS officer
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark Corp. has appointed Francesco Tinto as chief information & global business services (GBS) officer, effective March 9, 2026. Tinto will report to Russ Torres, president and chief operating officer, and will lead the company's Information Technology and GBS organizations. He brings over 30 years of technology leadership experience from companies including Advantage Solutions, Walgreens Boots Alliance, and Kraft Heinz, and will contribute to Kimberly-Clark's Powering Care strategy.

feed.title: Beam Global (BEEM) Advances Off-Grid EV Charging and Autonomous Vehicle Technology
feed.summary: Beam Global (BEEM) has partnered with HEVO Inc. to launch the first integrated autonomous wireless charging system, powered by EV ARC off-grid solar EV charging, targeting the autonomous vehicle sector. The company also secured an order for its patented battery products from a drone company for various robotic applications. Beam Global is recognized as a clean technology company focusing on sustainable, rapidly deployed solar-powered EV charging solutions.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance raises $5.7B in tax credit capital
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance successfully raised $5.7 billion in third-party capital in 2025, marking a historic high for the company. This capital supported 109 transactions across affordable housing, renewable energy, and economic development projects, financing over 6,800 affordable housing units and 5.2 gigawatts of clean energy capacity. The achievement highlights the financial stability of U.S. Bancorp, along with recent positive analyst upgrades and strategic executive changes.

feed.title: McKesson CFO to retire, replacement named
feed.summary: McKesson CFO Britt Vitalone is retiring after 20 years with the company. Kenny Cheung will officially take over as the new Chief Financial Officer in May. The transition occurs as McKesson's headquarters moved to DFW from Northern California.

feed.title: East Ohio Named KeyBank's 2025 "Market of the Year"
feed.summary: KeyBank's East Ohio market has been recognized as the bank's 2025 "Market of the Year" for its comprehensive growth and client-centric approach. The award acknowledges the outstanding service and collaboration among KeyBank's lines of business in the Akron/Canton area. This achievement was celebrated at KeyBank's annual Chairman's Awards in Cleveland, with the East Ohio market president Joe Daleiden receiving a traveling crystal trophy.

feed.title: Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp. (AQN) Q4 & Full Year 2025 Earnings: Revenue Hits $630.7M
feed.summary: Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp. (AQN) reported strong Q4 and full-year 2025 results, with Q4 adjusted EPS of $0.06 and revenue of $630.7M both beating analyst estimates. The company completed a transformational shift to a pure-play regulated utility, selling its renewable energy business and using the proceeds to reduce debt. CEO Rod West reaffirmed the 2026 adjusted net EPS outlook and outlined a $3.2 billion capital plan for 2026-2028, underscoring investor confidence in its "Back to Basics" strategy.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Technology Companies: Ooma (OOMA), Broadcom (AVGO) and Leidos Holdings (LDOS)
feed.summary: Analysts have issued bullish ratings for Ooma (OOMA), Broadcom (AVGO), and Leidos Holdings (LDOS) in the Technology sector. Lake Street reiterated a Buy rating for Ooma, Deutsche Bank for Broadcom, and Jefferies for Leidos Holdings, with all three companies showing consensus strong buy or moderate buy ratings and significant upside potential from their current prices.

feed.title: How Zip Helped T-Mobile Transform its Procurement
feed.summary: T-Mobile's CPO, Michael Simpson, detailed how the company modernized its procurement operations by implementing Zip's orchestration platform. This shift enabled T-Mobile to move away from legacy ERP systems, simplifying transactions for its 30,000 suppliers and managing $35 billion in spend. The transformation focused on making procurement a strategic value-add partner rather than merely a transaction-heavy department, emphasizing business process change and a unified gateway for all procurement activities.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: Olympian Alysa Liu's Message for International Women's Day
feed.summary: The NYSE issued a pre-market update featuring Olympian Alysa Liu's message for International Women's Day and Georgie Dickens of Women in Leadership Global discussing the holiday's significance. Robinhood CFO Shiv Verma is also planned to speak about the launch of Robinhood Ventures Fund I. The update highlighted market choppiness due to events in Iran and their impact on energy supply.

feed.title: Intercontinental Exchange Plans to Launch Four Container Freight Futures Indexed to Nyshex Freight Indices
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE) announced its plan to launch four new container freight futures contracts. These futures will be indexed to the New York Shipping Exchange's (NYSHEX) Freight Indices, which are calculated by ICE Data Services and serve as benchmarks for containerized freight pricing. The contracts, covering key shipping routes between the U.S., Asia, and Europe, are slated for an April 7, 2026 launch, pending regulatory approval, and aim to help the market manage container freight risk.

feed.title: Kimberly-Clark (KMB) Appoints New Chief Information Officer
feed.summary: Kimberly-Clark (KMB) has appointed Francesco Tinto as its new Chief Information & Global Business Services Officer, effective March 9. Tinto, previously Chief Digital Officer at Advantage Solutions, will report to Russ Torres and join the executive leadership team. Kimberly-Clark, a leading manufacturer in tissue and hygiene, currently has a market capitalization of $34.82 billion and operates within the Consumer Defensive sector.

feed.title: Connecticut Land-Clearing Contractor Adds Second Astec-Peterson Grinder
feed.summary: Connecticut Clearing, a land-clearing business owned by Dean Smith, has acquired a second Astec-Peterson horizontal grinder, a 5710E model, from Barry Equipment. This addition allows the company to handle a greater volume of grinding work and conduct full-scale land-clearing operations more efficiently across various project types in Connecticut. The new grinder is significantly more productive than the previous model, enhancing the company's capability to process wood debris, stumps, and root balls on-site.

feed.title: IQVIA Holdings (IQV) Rating Upgraded by Barclays to Overweight
feed.summary: Barclays has upgraded its rating for IQVIA Holdings (IQV) from Equal-Weight to Overweight, maintaining a price target of USD 210.00. This upgrade comes amidst varying analyst opinions, with other firms recently initiating coverage or adjusting price targets. The average target price from 22 analysts is $237.66, suggesting a significant upside from its current price, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $263.06.

feed.title: DOW Stock Upgraded to Overweight by JP Morgan, Price Target Raised
feed.summary: JP Morgan upgraded Dow (DOW) from "Neutral" to "Overweight" and significantly raised its price target from $26.00 to $40.00, a 53.85% increase. This follows several other recent analyst upgrades and price target adjustments for DOW. The average analyst target for DOW is $30.26, while GuruFocus estimates a fair value of $39.31, suggesting a potential upside of 16.58%.

feed.title: Ex-Equifax employee alleges firm allowed fraudulent access to credit file
feed.summary: A former Equifax employee has filed a class-action lawsuit against Equifax Canada Co. and Equifax Inc., claiming the credit bureau allowed a third-party partner, Borrowell, to access his credit file without consent based on a fraudulent account. The application alleges Equifax's automated matching mechanisms approved access using partial, inaccurate, or outdated information without proper identity verification. This case highlights the importance for investors affected by the CIRO data breach to regularly review their credit reports.

feed.title: Wall Street Analysts Are Bullish on Top Technology Picks
feed.summary: Wall Street analysts are showing bullish sentiment towards several technology companies, including Leidos Holdings (LDOS), CPS Technologies (CPSH), and Broadcom (AVGO). Analysts from Jefferies, Roth MKM, and Deutsche Bank reiterated Buy ratings for these companies, setting price targets that suggest significant upside from current levels. The article highlights the individual analyst's ratings, success rates, and the consensus price targets for each stock.

feed.title: Atlantic Union appoints CFO
feed.summary: Atlantic Union Bankshares Corp. has appointed Alexander D. Dodd as its new CFO. Dodd, who previously served as deputy CFO and EVP of TD Bank Group, will succeed Robert M. Gorman, who is retiring but will remain as an executive advisor through September 30. John C. Asbury, president and CEO of Atlantic Union Bankshares, highlighted Dodd's extensive experience in enterprise finance across U.S. and Canadian banking.

feed.title: Huntington National Bank opens Winston-Salem, North Carolina branch
feed.summary: Huntington National Bank has opened a full-service branch in Winston-Salem, North Carolina, as part of its plan to establish approximately 55 branches in the Carolinas. This marks the bank's sixth branch in the region across five markets. The expansion follows Huntington's acquisition of Cadence Bank in early February 2026, which added around 400 branches in the South.

feed.title: BBNX INVESTOR NOTICE: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP Launches Investigation into Beta Bionics
feed.summary: Faruqi & Faruqi, LLP has launched an investigation into Beta Bionics (NASDAQ: BBNX) following a significant stock drop. This comes after the company disclosed preliminary fourth-quarter 2025 performance metrics that fell short of market expectations, specifically concerning new patient starts for its iLet automated insulin delivery system, leading to investor concerns about adoption and revenue growth.

feed.title: J&J Wins Third National Priority Approval for Multiple Myeloma Combo
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson has secured a third National Priority Approval from the FDA for its combination regimen of Tecvayli and Darzalex for the second-line treatment of multiple myeloma. This expedited approval, granted via a Commissioner's National Priority Voucher, was issued just 55 days after J&J's application filing. The combination therapy reportedly improved progression-free survival by 83% and reduced the risk of death by 54% in the Phase 3 MajesTEC-3 study.

feed.title: Traffic Will Be A Mess As Xcel Energy Moves Wind Turbines
feed.summary: Xcel Energy will be transporting generators and turbines across Northern Colorado this weekend, starting Saturday morning around 7 AM. These slow-moving vehicles, traveling at about 5 MPH, will cause significant traffic delays on I-25, Highway 287, and Colorado Highway 66, particularly near Longmont and Lyons. This is the first of four planned moves as part of Colorado's 2024 Clean Energy Plan to add 6,100 megawatts of clean generation capacity to the St. Vrain Generating Station.

feed.title: BMO supporting women’s health through conferences, resources
feed.summary: BMO is enhancing its support for women's health through information sessions and an expanded resource hub, launching a women's cardiovascular health conference in March. These initiatives, developed with Cleveland Clinic Canada and BMO's Alliance for Women, have seen significant employee interest, covering topics from cancer and fertility to menopause and neurodiversity. BMO is also creating a dedicated women's health page within its wellness hub, providing session recordings and resources.

feed.title: Why Microsoft's Heavy AI Spending Is Driving Profits, Not Destroying Them (NASDAQ:MSFT)
feed.summary: Microsoft (MSFT) is rated a Strong BUY due to its aggressive AI-focused capital expenditure, which is fueling robust revenue and margin expansion. This CapEx primarily supports AI infrastructure for both proprietary products like Copilot and client solutions, effectively locking customers into the Microsoft ecosystem. Despite high capital intensity and competitive pressures, the Intelligent Cloud and Productivity & Business Processes segments are showing double-digit growth and margin expansion, positioning MSFT for continued financial success.

feed.title: Academy Sports + Outdoors Increases Quarterly Cash Dividend
feed.summary: Academy Sports and Outdoors, Inc. announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.15 per share for the fiscal quarter ended January 31, 2026. This represents a 15 percent increase from the previous dividend, marking the fourth consecutive year of dividend growth. The dividend is payable on April 10, 2026, to stockholders of record as of March 20, 2026.

feed.title: Haywood Habitat for Humanity to Receive 2026 National Neighborhood Promise Grant
feed.summary: Haywood Habitat for Humanity has been awarded a $250,000 National Neighborhood Promise grant from the Republic Services Charitable Foundation and Republic Services Inc. This funding will be used to create a nature walking path and play area connecting Habitat’s Chestnut Park neighborhood to the surrounding community. This project aims to foster connection, recreation, and a stronger sense of neighborhood, with Republic Services employees also volunteering to help bring the initiative to life.

feed.title: Honeywell Overhauls Liquidity With New $7B Revolvers and $6B Term Loan; Ends Prior Facilities
feed.summary: Honeywell has significantly restructured its liquidity framework by securing new credit facilities, including a $3.0 billion 364-day revolver, a $4.0 billion five-year revolver, and a $6.0 billion short-term term loan. Simultaneously, it terminated its previous 2024 and 2025 credit facilities. This overhaul, which also includes separate facilities for its Aerospace spin-off, enhances liquidity and flexibility for corporate needs, tender offers, and redemptions.

feed.title: Avantor (NYSE:AVTR) Earns Underweight Rating from Barclays
feed.summary: Barclays has reiterated an "underweight" rating on Avantor (NYSE:AVTR) with an $8.50 price target, despite the company slightly beating recent earnings expectations. The stock is trading near its 52-week low with a negative P/E, and concerns exist about future earnings given forecast cuts by other research firms. Notably, two directors recently purchased a combined 150,000 shares, showing insider confidence amidst mixed analyst sentiment and high institutional ownership.

feed.title: What's Next: Hewlett Packard's Earnings Preview
feed.summary: Hewlett Packard (NYSE: HPE) is scheduled to release its quarterly earnings on March 9, 2026. Analysts anticipate an EPS of $0.55, and investors will be looking for the company to beat estimates and provide positive guidance. The article also provides an overview of HPE's financial performance, including its market capitalization, revenue growth, net margin, and debt management.

feed.title: Cyber firm Fortinet to stream March 10 investor briefing live online
feed.summary: Fortinet (NASDAQ: FTNT) announced it will host a live investor briefing webcast on Tuesday, March 10, 2026, at 3:00 PM PT. The briefing will take place during its Accelerate 2026 conference and is intended for financial analysts and investors. It will cover security and product topics presented at the conference.

feed.title: 4,000 WSU engineering students to train on industry Fluke tools
feed.summary: Fluke Corporation (NYSE: FTV) and Washington State University (WSU) have announced a strategic partnership to equip over 4,000 engineering students with industry-standard test and measurement tools across five campuses. This collaboration includes donating professional equipment, establishing the Fluke Engineering Lab at WSU Everett, and continuing a mentorship program to bridge the gap between academic preparation and workforce demands. The initiative aims to enhance students' hands-on experience in areas like predictive maintenance and energy efficiency, preparing them for careers in manufacturing and industrial sectors.

feed.title: The Metals Company and ASP Isotopes Interviews to Air on the RedChip Small Stocks, Big Money(TM) Show on Bloomberg TV
feed.summary: RedChip Companies will broadcast interviews with The Metals Company (Nasdaq:TMC) and ASP Isotopes (Nasdaq:ASPI) on its "Small Stocks, Big Money™" show on Bloomberg TV. The interviews, featuring TMC's CFO discussing critical mineral dependence and ASP Isotopes' CEO on stable isotopes and nuclear fuels, will air on Saturday, March 7, at 7 p.m. Eastern Time. Both companies are RedChip clients, and the discussions will cover their strategic market positions and advancements.

feed.title: GreenRow turns SoHo shop into a lived-in home for sustainable design fans
feed.summary: Williams-Sonoma's GreenRow has opened its first brick-and-mortar store in SoHo, New York City, designed to resemble a lived-in home to showcase its sustainably crafted home furnishings. The store emphasizes quality, sustainability, and timeless design through products made with responsibly sourced materials. GreenRow aims to provide an immersive retail experience for customers who care about how products are made and how they integrate into their homes.

feed.title: Price Over Earnings Overview: WR Berkley
feed.summary: WR Berkley (NYSE: WRB) shares are trading at $68.61 after a 1.38% drop, with a 1.19% decrease over the past month but a 10.50% increase over the past year. The company's P/E ratio of 15.63 is higher than the Insurance industry's aggregate P/E of 12.23, suggesting it might be overvalued or expected to perform better than its peers. However, investors are cautioned to use the P/E ratio alongside other financial metrics and qualitative factors for a comprehensive analysis.

feed.title: U.S. Bank Raises $5.7 Billion Through Tax Credit Syndications in 2025
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp Impact Finance reported a record-breaking 2025, raising approximately $5.7 billion in third-party capital through tax credit syndications from 58 institutional investors across 109 transactions. These funds are designated for financing affordable housing, renewable energy, and economic development projects, marking the highest annual total in the company's history. The firm also surpassed $7 billion in tax credit transfers since the Inflation Reduction Act of 2023, indicating significant growth and market maturity in tax credit solutions.

feed.title: Form 424B2 JPMORGAN CHASE & CO
feed.summary: JPMorgan Chase Financial Company LLC has filed a Form 424B2 with the SEC for structured investments consisting of $3,841,000 Auto Callable Contingent Interest Notes due March 8, 2029. These notes are linked to the performance of the Nasdaq-100 Index, Russell 2000 Index, and State Street SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF, with automatic call features and contingent interest payments based on the performance of the underlying assets. Investors face the risk of principal loss if the underlying assets fall below specified trigger values.

feed.title: Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. Investigated by the Portnoy Law Firm
feed.summary: The Portnoy Law Firm has launched an investigation into potential securities fraud involving Zimmer Biomet Holdings, Inc. (NYSE:ZBH). This action follows a 15.2% stock price drop on November 5, 2025, after Zimmer Biomet reported weaker-than-expected Q3 financial results and lowered its revenue growth forecast due to softness in Latin America, emerging European markets, and a slowdown in the U.S. revision market for hips and knees. Investors are encouraged to contact the law firm to discuss their legal options for recovering losses.

feed.title: Honeywell seeks up to $16B to finance planned Aerospace spin-off
feed.summary: Honeywell announced that its Aerospace division has launched a private offering of up to $16 billion in senior notes to finance its planned spin-off, expected in Q3 2026. Additionally, Aerospace secured $4 billion in revolving credit facilities. Proceeds from some "New Money Notes" will go to Honeywell as a cash distribution, while "Exchange Notes" will satisfy existing debt obligations without providing cash to Aerospace.

feed.title: Assessing Apollo Global Management’s Valuation After New Epstein-Related Class Action Lawsuits
feed.summary: New class action lawsuits against Apollo Global Management regarding Epstein-related contacts are impacting investor sentiment and the company's valuation. While Apollo's P/E ratio is near the industry average, a Discounted Cash Flow model suggests the stock is significantly undervalued, raising questions about whether current market adjustments present a buying opportunity or reflect future growth concerns. The article advises investors to consider both financial metrics and ongoing legal risks.

feed.title: ASML Holding (ASML) Plans to Expand Beyond Their Core EUV Lithography
feed.summary: ASML Holding N.V. is looking to expand beyond its core EUV lithography technology into the rapidly growing AI chip market, according to CTO Marco Pieters. The company, which holds a monopoly on essential EUV lithography machines, plans to develop tools for advanced packaging, a critical process for AI processors. This strategic move aims to leverage their expertise for future growth in the semiconductor industry.

feed.title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) Down 16% Since FQ4 2025 Earnings, Here’s Why
feed.summary: Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) has seen a 16% stock decline since its fiscal Q4 2025 earnings report despite exceeding revenue and EPS estimates. Analysts maintain a bullish outlook, with a significant majority recommending a "Buy" rating and forecasting substantial upside. The company reported strong year-over-year revenue growth driven by its data center, client, and gaming segments.

feed.title: Aramark Celebrates Standout Partners Powering Local Restaurant Row in 2025
feed.summary: Aramark is celebrating the standout partners who will be powering the Local Restaurant Row in 2025. This initiative highlights the company's commitment to collaborating with local businesses to enhance its service offerings.

feed.title: Trigran Investments Inc. Increases Stock Position in Barrett Business Services, Inc. $BBSI
feed.summary: Trigran Investments Inc. significantly increased its stake in Barrett Business Services, Inc. (NASDAQ:BBSI) by 121.3% in the third quarter, now owning 399,686 shares valued at $17.7 million, making BBSI its 13th largest holding. This increase comes as institutional investors collectively own about 86.8% of the company. Barrett Business Services recently reported Q3 earnings of $0.64 EPS, in line with estimates, and a revenue increase of 5.3% year-over-year, alongside announcing a quarterly dividend of $0.08.
